solution:
def palindromePartition(s: str, k: int) -> int:
    n = len(s)
    dp = [0] * (n + 1)

    for i in range(1, n + 1):
        for j in range(i, n + 1):
            if s[i - 1] == s[j]:
                dp[i] = min(dp[i], dp[j] + 1)
                if k > dp[i]:
                    dp[i] = n

    return dp[k - 1]

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n^2)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW

Explanation:
We use a dynamic programming approach to solve the problem. We create a 2D array (dp) with dimensions (n+1)x(n+1), where n is the length of the string s. We iterate through the string and check if consecutive characters are the same. If they are, we update the dp[i] value to be the minimum of dp[i] and dp[j] + 1, where j is the index of the next character. If k is greater than dp[i], we update dp[i] to n, as we cannot create k non-empty palindromes. Finally, we return the value at dp[k-1].
